review_title: Highly flexible and user-friendly collaboration platform
description: Podio has been a game-changer for our team. Its flexibility allows us to setup workflows exactly to our processes. Collaboration across teams has improved significantly, and integrations with other tools have streamlined our daily operations. The value for money and ease of use make it an excellent choice for project and task management.
author: Ansar M.
pros: Flexibility, ease of use, excellent team collaboration, customizable workflows, and powerful API for external integrations.
cons: Nothing significant so far, the platform meets ours & our client needs very well. The UI could be more modern, but it works really well and doesn’t affect functionality.

description: Growing to use the system more for real estate but still awaiting a return call or email to increase usage
author: Stephanie B.
pros: Collaboration ease, organizational convenience, growing add-ons. My husband uses the system much more
cons: Getting a response from their team is a hope and dream. Even if you email. My assistant has been awaiting on boarding after contacting with no resolve

review_title: Tried Podio for Six Months and Redesigned With a Desktop App
description: We tried using it for 6 months and abandoned it for a traditional desktop application.
author: John D.
pros: Many nice features includes out-of-the-box such as maps and simple data validations.
cons: Poor options for backing up data. Third-party options seemed of questionable reliability and not automated.

description: I love the automation features, as well as the ability to integrate RightSignature, Dynamic Forms, etc. It's really saves us a LOT of time automating tasks and emails!!
author: Jen P.
pros: The customer service response time from our Podio rep is amazing! Jacquelyn is always available to help and super fast to respond and solve our problems when we need her!
cons: Lack of reporting options. It would be very helpful if Podio had some better printing and reporting options.

description: Like that it is a crm all in one, coming from working with applications like salesforce. Excited to hear the incorporation of AI to the platform.
author: Craig J.
pros: Ease of use and ability to customize. Like that you can see calls and last comments tagged to your email automatically.
cons: Alerts and ease of making reminder more automated. But can i do more, with the app, that is my limitation.

description: Overall it is very seemless to use and very user friendly I just used it for 3 months and I can say it is easy to get used to
author: Mac T.
pros: The cost I don't have an idea because I'm from IT, haven't had any issues yet so I haven't contacted customer support, the ease of use is very good easy to navigate and get used to all workflows, task management is very easy and very flexible on navigation, project collaboration is a good feature and gives us flexibility on tasks.
cons: Just a very few automation, some of our applications are not yet integrated to podio so we have to use zapier to get that running
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

description: My experience with Podio has been great. I like the functions and the ability to collaborate and assign tasks to other team members.
author: spencer P.
pros: I like the collaboration and customization abilities that Podio offers. Podio is easy to figure out and learn by yourself. It is Facebook for businesses.
cons: Reports can be hard to run and confusing. I also dislike the ability to turn off commenting in certain areas of Podio.

review_title: Great CRM but always room for improvement
description: It’s pretty good overall for the price can’t beat it for a basic CRM up into an advanced CRM. It still works very well for So operations to smaller operations. It can be easily scaled to be used in large operations.
author: Mark W.
pros: The system is adaptability to integrate API‘s and workflows in a relatively easy manner. I also enjoy the ease of creating new workspaces.
cons: I don’t like that. It’s hard to incorporate automated follow ups as well as certain apps don’t integrate within the CRM.

review_title: Give Podio a chance - thank me later
description: I like how you can get in out, I like the search feature and I like how I can go back and edit and add things that I learned about clients. I’m also inspired knowing that it can do so much more than the basic features I’m using now.
author: Michelle S.
pros: Tremendous support. All their team members know EVERY nuance. It’s been a great way to track tasks and it’s my go to CRM.
cons: It’s not really known as a CRM so I think I may be taking shortcuts and not taking advantage of many features and benefits.

review_title: A great tool for organization, project management and collaboration.
description: Podio has been at the core of basically all my workflow for the last 12 years or so and it has been a lifesaver.
author: Fabio M.
pros: Customizability and flexibility; Task management; Integration with google drive; Team cooperation and coordination
cons: Some automations could be easier (buttons) and some customization could also be easier (different item layouts).

description: Overall Podio is so much better than every other CRM we have used. I've recommended it to everyone in my industry.
author: Ryan J.
pros: I love how we can customize everything and how many integrations are supported.
cons: Not a fan of the UI. Looks outdated and can be confusing for new users.

description: My overall experience with Podio is very good and I am really happy to use. Exporting report is much easier to find.
author: Mary Muhha M.
pros: it is easy to use and consolidation is much good. task management is clearly highlighted. It is really value of money.
cons: The only thing that i dont like is small bugs and issues sometimes that can disrupt the podio tasks.

description: My overall experience with Podio is great. I started with Podio while running a real estate investment company. I now utilized it with my roofing company as well. The advanced automations with globiflow make it a force to be recon with in the CRM space.
author: Cody W.
pros: The ability to customize Podio in order for it to help scale your business is what I like most about Podio. All of the latest integrations are keeping Podio at the top of the list for CRMs.
cons: There are very few times it occurs, but when the site/status goes down during the middle of the work day it can wreck havoc on a business that relies on it heavily. Over the last 6 years I have only had it affect me 3-4 times.

review_title: Good- but look at the total price.
description: I liked Podio, and if you don't need a lot of bells and whistles the basic version may be ideal for you.
author: Ann B.
pros: I really liked how Podio integrated with Google Drive and Dropbox. it made it super easy to share files. I think it's one of the more intuitive platforms for users.
cons: It's NOT that intuitive for those setting up the tasks and workflows. It may take them awhile to get things working the way you want, and if you only have one person managing the team, and they leave, you may be in dire straits if you need to make changes. I also thought Podio became more expensive quickly. the basic version is affordable but every additional feature costs more and then you are paying A LOT. And considering all the time for set-up, you may be hesitant to leave. I needed a better tracking and reporting system than what I could get from Podio, and price was getting high, so I switched

author: Sylla N.
pros: Highly customizable, intuitive, and collaborative. Strong integration and automation features.
cons: Less advanced features compared to competitors, lag with too many customizations.
author_duration: Used the software for: I used a free trial

description: We loved working with it. It was such a great tool to use. However, we did in the end switch away to a more traditional PM system, just because the setup and structuring was a little bit of overkill for what we needed as we grew.
author: Melanie P.
pros: Podio is this fantastic gem that you don't see in other project management, task management or workflow management systems. It's quite literally limitless. You can create unique systems and flows for your own needs without a ton of tech-knowledge (but you certainly need some). It's a really neat way to create the EXACT workflow and data management pieces that you're looking for.
cons: Podio is not simple to set up or implement. It takes time, planning and a decent amount of tech understanding. You don't need to know how to program, but it's certainly not for the faint of heart with the setup. However once you get it - it's very cool. I honestly don't have too much more that's negative to say about it.

review_title: Podio - Flexible collaboration tool for teams
description: It's difficult to describe Podio in a few sentences as it's flexibility and extensibility through the addition of apps from the library or creating your own means that it can be used for many different things. We've used it for project management and as a simple space to hold and share information when several external parties are involved. It means everyone can see what needs to be shared and current status with compromising internal systems and worrying about correct security settings for various parties. Podio isn't always first choice for team collaboration as there may already be solutions in place that clients prefer (for example, Microsoft Teams), but it is a quick way to pull disparate people and data together and get working quickly. As with all collaboration tools, it needs a bit of planning to configure it best for the type of project you want to use it for, but once you have used it a couple of times setup should be quick. For 'standard' users who have a degree of competence with collaboration software, the interface is intuitive and they should get up to speed pretty quickly. If you work in an Agile environment, all the tools are there to configure an Agile project and get started quickly. Some people love Podio immediately, others don't get it. Try out with a couple of small projects to get familiar with it; keep it as simple as possible - Podio's greatest strength is that you don't need to add more features/apps than are suited to the job.
author: Colin M.
pros: Fast to configure for team collaboration on a variety of tasks - from the simple to fully featured Agile project management. Adding only the apps you need means you can have a flexible and bespoke approach to each project depending on the requirements. Easy to share with external users.
cons: The flexibility that can be a strength can also be a weakness. There needs to be strong leadership over configuration and apps included. The number of apps that can be added and the configurable options can lead to unnecessary complexity in the hands of inexperienced users. If you have complex security requirements, configuration can be a pain.

description: I'm using the free version, so for me it's a free project management tool that can be customized to track any type of project or process. I can assign and schedule tasks for any user in the workspace.
author: Verified Reviewer
pros: It can do anything! There are pre-developed apps and packages of apps that you can add to your workspace to get started quickly. Those modules can be modified to meet your needs. OR, you can create your own apps to serve your own purposes. I've created my own apps for a variety of workspaces and have found it can do almost anything! So far we've used it for lead development, website development/QA, order tracking, and customer feedback handling. Love that we can assigne and schedule tasks for any user in the workspace. We're a small team, so the free version has been able to meet our needs as well.
cons: It can be time consuming to figure out how build your own applications. The pre-built apps are great to start with, but then you have to visualize how to modify them to meet you unique needs. Not bad, just a learning curve. Also, the per user per month expenses can add up quickly if you need the more advanced features.

review_title: Podio is great for organizing and storing information
description: Podio has helped a lot in organizing the day-to-day activities of the company I work in, facilitating communication, file storage and data control.
author: Emanuelly N.
pros: Podio is a great tool to use for work, as it is possible to create tasks and mark the people responsible and this helps a lot in the organization and retention of information due to the fact that in these tasks it is possible to attach documents, in addition it is great to create events assisting in day-to-day meetings to have control of the people present and it is great to create a list of contacts, to store information of customers and distributors of the company.
cons: I believe that Podio could improve the way of notifying the person who is marked in the tasks, it could be possible to create a table with all the tasks that the users have to do, in order to better organize and control the deadlines.

review_title: Podio is great place to divide your workspaces
description: Overall, Podio is very user friendly. I'm unsure of the exact cost as the [SENSITIVE CONTENT HIDDEN] handles that. I like the functionality of Podio in that we can have employees submit their applications right in the platform and then build their employee files off of that application.
author: Barbara M.
pros: I like having all the different workspaces to separate our different company locations and depts. I like the ease of setting an employee up or removing an employee and the ease of inviting an employee to a workspace.
cons: I don't like if I have an employee who is no longer at one work space and moved to another, I can't easily move their workspace location. I have to create separate workspaces in different workspaces for an employee.

review_title: A great tool to keep all your projects in order, serves it's purpose.
description: In the time that I have been using it, it solve many communication problems, I was able to record and track each action or request that is made throughout, for example, a month, and it allows an easy way to remember and collect the data of each last month.
author: Augusto R.
pros: First of all, it adapts to the user in an amazing way, I really liked that. Whether for a small company or a very large one, and not only companies, also anyone who wants to have their projects organized. The cost is affordable, makes it accessible to companies of all kinds. Finally, I must say that it’s great to share real-time progress of projects with clients to confidently engage them in work productivity.
cons: They could improve document sharing, which is a bit tricky. They should improve the mobile app. It could also be integrated with other apps to sharing over there.

review_title: Very flexible software, but increasingly unreliable
description: Overall, pretty good for a budget CRM. But it feels like a platform with its better days behind it.
author: Verified Reviewer
pros: Podio is designed for project managers, but we used it as our CRM - which shows how flexible it is. It worked well, the UI is nice, the workflows save you a lot of time and you can link up items well for automation (e.g. if you close a deal, mark the associated company item as 'customer'). The reporting worked quite well too. You can pull data into reports and create nice dashboards. And you can export to Excel.
cons: Citrix aren't investing in Podio - it looks the same as when we started using it years ago. It's also increasingly unreliable - there have been numerous all-day outages in 2018. This is frustrating when you depend on Podio to run your business. We have since moved onto a different CRM software because of these issues.

description: I use Podio as the main frame for our internal communications software. We are branching out to 'share' items to both suppliers and clients that we mark apps and workspaces specifically without proprietary information. This way we can all communicate in a network without causing too much of an overlap in other softwares.
author: Verified Reviewer
pros: Javascript based easy calculations fields. Relationship fields allow easy access to 'like' items that don't follow the same workflow. Globiflow integrations opens up 'get' 'post' and'if this then that' features that cut down on tedious work. Podio Super menu chrome extension is extremely helpful for entities with a lot of workspaces/apps.
cons: Without extension you cannot mark several items in table format. You have to go into each individual item separately (or export to excel update and them import) which is a massive pain.

description: hate it will be looking to creating my own app this company is a horrible company and hope they go under one day
author: Verified Reviewer
pros: some of the uploading space and its funny they make you do min words lol
cons: almost everything cust service is horrible and they are very over priced
